HEADLINE: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles Thousands of people protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump marched in Southern California on Sunday. Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked a major freeway. Protests were also held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. CORRECTS NAME OF THE FREEWAY - Law enforcement personnel stage in front of a group of demonstrators who shut down the 101 freeway during a protest calling for immigration reform Sunday, Feb. 2, 2025, in Los Angeles. parsed_output.summary: Thousands of protesters in Los Angeles blocked the 101 freeway as part of nationwide demonstrations against President Donald Trump's planned mass deportations and to call for immigration reform. The Los Angeles protest saw significant law enforcement presence as marchers gathered downtown, held signs, and waved flags. Similar protests took place in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. The event underscored widespread opposition to the administration's immigration policies and disrupted major traffic arteries. The Los Angeles demonstration occurred on February 2, 2025.
key_facts: Thousands protested planned mass deportations by President Donald Trump.
Demonstrators blocked the 101 freeway in downtown Los Angeles.
Protests also occurred in Dallas and McAllen, Texas.
Law enforcement staged significant presence at the Los Angeles protest.
Protesters called for immigration reform and opposed deportations on February 2, 2025.
